steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the subject of the when-clause: “When ______ debuted in January 1983 as part of the exhibit Women of Clay.” The main clause identifies the antecedent: “the artworks.” The skill tested is pronoun-antecedent agreement in number.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — they: a plural subject pronoun that agrees with the plural antecedent “the artworks.” ✓
  • Choice B — that: a relative pronoun typically used to introduce a relative clause, not as a standalone subject pronoun referring back to a plural noun. It does not agree with “the artworks” in the way the sentence requires. ✗
  • Choice C — it: a singular pronoun; violates pronoun-antecedent agreement because “the artworks” is plural. ✗
  • Choice D — this: a singular demonstrative pronoun; also fails to agree with the plural antecedent “the artworks.” ✗
A is correct.
